title: Target Sum
problem_statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer target, return the number of ways to assign a '+' or '-' sign to each element such that the resulting sum equals target.

examples.explanation: There are five ways to assign signs to get sum 3.
Three sign assignments yield the sum 3.

intuition: Convert the problem to selecting a subset of numbers as positive so that the difference between selected and unselected sums equals the target.

approaches.name: Brute Force Sign Assignment
approaches.description: Try every sign combination using recursion, summing values with + or - and counting matches to target.
approaches.why_not: Exponential in n; acceptable only for very small arrays.

approaches.name: Memoized Recursion (Top-Down DP)
approaches.description: Memoize the number of ways to reach each sum at each index to avoid recomputing overlapping states.
approaches.why_not: Still recursive, but avoids the exponential blow-up.

approaches.name: Subset Sum Transformation
approaches.description: Reformulate as counting subsets whose sum equals (total + target) / 2, then use 1D DP to count subset sums.
approaches.why_not: This is the standard optimal solution for the transformed problem.

interview_qa.q: How is Target Sum transformed to subset sum?
interview_qa.a: Let P be the sum of numbers assigned '+', and N be the sum assigned '-'. Then P - N = target and P + N = total, so P = (total + target)/2.
If total + target is odd or |target| > total, no sign assignment can satisfy the equation.

interview_qa.q: Why iterate the dp array backward?
interview_qa.a: To ensure each number is only counted once per subset.
Zeros double the number of ways for each valid subset sum because +0 and -0 are equivalent but counted separately in the original problem.
